goal: Fix generator/parser round-trip asymmetry for hash-comment semantic headers: HeaderGenerator.formatAsComments writes '#'-line @coderef-semantic headers for Python (and other hash-comment languages), but semantic-header-parser detectHeaderBlock only recognizes /** */ blocks, // line comments, and triple-quote docstrings - so stamped Python files stay headerStatus=missing. Proven on primary-sources 2026-07-06 (DISPATCH-2026-07-06-CODEREF-CORE-001): 520 stamped .py files parse to 0 defined; all 3764 missing elements are .py while JS/TS/TSX parse 100%. Fix: add hash-line detection to detectHeaderBlock (mirror the // branch with # prefix, preserving shebang+PEP263 coding-cookie handling). Round-trip contract: write then parse => defined.

tasks.description: READ src/pipeline/semantic-header-parser.ts (detectHeaderBlock, ~lines 56-95) and src/semantic/header-generator.ts (formatAsComments hash-comment branch, ~line 293) to lock the exact '#'-line header shape the writer emits for Python before touching the parser.

READ the existing header-parser test surfaces (__tests__/pipeline/output-validation-semantic-headers.test.ts and any semantic-header-parser unit tests) to find where round-trip coverage belongs and which fixtures exist.

AUTHOR a FAILING round-trip test: HeaderGenerator writes a '#'-style @coderef-semantic header into .py fixture content (three variants: plain, with shebang line, with PEP263 coding cookie '# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-'), then semantic-header-parser must detect the block (headerStatus=defined). Run it to prove red before the fix.

EDIT src/pipeline/semantic-header-parser.ts detectHeaderBlock: add a hash-line-comment branch mirroring the existing '//' branch (match leading '(?:#.*\n)+' block, strip '#'-prefixes, require SEMANTIC_MARKER_RE in the stripped body). MUST NOT consume the shebang line (existing '#!' strip runs first) and MUST tolerate a PEP263 coding-cookie line adjacent to the header block. Do not alter the /** */, //, or docstring branches.
RUN the pipeline test suite (npx vitest run __tests__/pipeline plus the new round-trip test): new test green, zero regressions on block-comment, line-comment, and docstring header forms.

RUN npm run build, then E2E-verify at the CLI surface on a temp Python fixture project: populate-coderef --source-headers followed by a second measuring populate-coderef run; assert .py header coverage > 0 (round-trip closed end-to-end through dist/).
COMMIT the parser fix + tests on main by explicit path (/git-commit --paths src/pipeline/semantic-header-parser.ts <test files>); push via /git-commit --done or /git-land.

RUN a fresh measuring populate over C:/Users/willh/Desktop/CODEREF/PROJECTS/primary-sources (headers already stamped 2026-07-06; .coderef artifacts untracked): node dist/src/cli/populate.js . from the PS repo root using the rebuilt dist. No --source-headers needed. Precondition: confirm stamped '#' headers are still present in PS working tree (they are uncommitted); if PS reverted them, coordinate with PRIMARY-SOURCES before proceeding.

CHECK primary-sources .coderef/index.json + validation-report.json: previously-missing .py elements flip to defined (element-level coverage rises from 21.7% toward ~100%); JS/TS/TSX defined counts unchanged; total_elements does not regress; stale/partial counts do not spike.
COMMIT any execution notes under coderef/workorder/ by explicit path and report the coverage delta back (optional follow-up dispatch to PRIMARY-SOURCES with the new by_status breakdown).
